name: Koala B2B Intent API
what_it_does: B2B buyer intent and product-qualified lead (PQL) platform with REST API — identifying website visitors and product users showing buying intent, scoring accounts based on behavioral signals, and surfacing high-intent leads to sales teams via CRM integration.
use_cases: Querying product-qualified leads and intent scores for sales team prioritization
Syncing high-intent accounts from Koala to Salesforce/HubSpot via API webhook
Monitoring website visitor identification data for targeted outreach
Triggering sales alerts when target accounts show high intent signals
Building custom PQL workflows using Koala intent data via REST API
not_for: General marketing automation (Koala is sales intelligence, not email marketing)
Pure product analytics without commercial intent tracking
B2C consumer apps without enterprise sales motion
best_when: A product-led growth company wants to identify which product users or website visitors are most likely to convert, with CRM sync for sales team prioritization.
avoid_when: You don't have an enterprise sales motion, sell purely consumer, or need general marketing automation.

auth.notes: API key from Koala workspace. Bearer token auth. Workspace-scoped access. JavaScript SDK for frontend tracking. Webhook for intent signal events. No OAuth2 or fine-grained scopes.

pricing.notes: Free tier for early-stage companies. Volume-based pricing. US-based startup. Founded 2022. YC W22. Purpose-built for product-led growth. JavaScript tracking pixel for visitor identification. Salesforce and HubSpot integration. Growing PLG community adoption.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for accounts, contacts, and intent signals. JavaScript SDK for frontend tracking. Bearer token auth. Webhook for high-intent events. Good documentation. Intent scores are ML-derived — query API to understand current scoring model. Privacy compliance important for visitor identification.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Account reads idempotent. Intent score queries idempotent. Webhook events are point-in-time signals. Contact management idempotent by identifier.
known_agent_gotchas: JavaScript tracking pixel required for visitor identification on website
Intent scores are ML-derived — scores change as user behavior changes
GDPR consent required for visitor identification in EU — implement consent mechanism
Third-party cookies phase-out affects some identification capabilities

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2 certified. GDPR compliant. US data residency. Bearer token auth. Visitor identification involves PII — GDPR consent required. Third-party data collection via tracking pixel — implement privacy-first approach.
